problem: Implement npm Trusted Publishing via GitHub OIDC for @projectxinnovation/helix-cli. The repo currently has no CI/CD workflows, no repository metadata in package.json, and the update mechanism is entirely GitHub-direct (npm install -g github:...). A publish workflow, package.json metadata, pack validation, and update path migration are all needed.

files.why: Missing 'repository' field required for npm Trusted Publishing. Contains bin mapping (hlx -> dist/index.js), files array (['dist']), prepare script, and version (1.2.0). No publishConfig or homepage fields. No runtime dependencies.
Build configuration: ESM (ES2022/Node16), rootDir=src, outDir=dist, strict mode, declaration generation. Build command is 'tsc'.
Excludes node_modules/ and dist/ from git. dist/ is generated at build time, which means the publish workflow must build before pack.
CLI entrypoint with shebang. Routes commands including 'update'. Auto-update check runs before command dispatch. This compiles to dist/index.js which is the bin target.

Defines CANONICAL_REPO_URL, CANONICAL_BRANCH, CANONICAL_REPO constants. Uses 'git ls-remote' to fetch remote SHA for update checks. Must be migrated to use npm registry version checks for npm-based updates.
Executes update via 'npm install -g github:Project-X-Innovation/helix-cli#main'. Must be migrated to 'npm install -g @projectxinnovation/helix-cli' for npm-based updates.

Orchestrates update flow: check remote SHA -> compare with local -> perform update -> validate install -> save metadata. References InstallSource mode='github'. Both runUpdate() and checkAutoUpdate() need migration.
Post-install validation: resolves npm root -g, checks dist/index.js exists, runs 'node <binTarget> --version'. This validation logic is reusable for publish-time validation.
Reads version from ../../package.json relative to dist/update/version.js at runtime. Must work correctly when installed from npm registry (package.json is included in published tarball at root).
Defines InstallSource type with mode: 'github' | 'unknown'. Needs 'npm' mode added. Config stored at ~/.hlx/config.json with installSource metadata.
Test file that compiles to dist/lib/flags.test.js. Currently included in published tarball via files:['dist']. Test files are unnecessary in published package.
Test file that compiles to dist/lib/resolve-ticket.test.js. Also included in published tarball. Same concern as flags.test.ts.
Does not exist. Must be created as the main deliverable — GitHub Actions workflow for npm Trusted Publishing with OIDC.
lockfileVersion 3, only devDependencies: @types/node and typescript. No runtime deps simplifies the publish.

facts: No .github directory exists — no CI/CD workflows of any kind
package.json has no 'repository' field — required for npm Trusted Publishing identity verification
package.json files field is ['dist'] which correctly limits published content to compiled output
bin field maps 'hlx' to 'dist/index.js' — the CLI entrypoint contract
dist/index.js has correct shebang: #!/usr/bin/env node
Build (npm run build / tsc) completes successfully with no errors
Tests (npm test) pass: 30/30, using node:test runner on compiled dist/**/*.test.js
npm pack --dry-run shows 77 files, 23.3kB packed — all under dist/ plus package.json
Test .d.ts and .test.js files are included in the tarball (dist/lib/flags.test.js, dist/lib/resolve-ticket.test.js) — unnecessary for installed CLI
No runtime dependencies — only devDependencies (@types/node, typescript)
No README file exists in the repo
prepare script runs 'npm run build' — triggers on npm install from GitHub but NOT on npm publish from registry
package.json version is 1.2.0
ESM module (type: 'module') with ES2022 target and Node16 module resolution
Node engine requirement: >=18
Update mechanism uses 'git ls-remote' for version checking and 'npm install -g github:...' for install
InstallSource.mode is typed as 'github' | 'unknown' — no 'npm' variant
Auto-update only runs for canonical GitHub installs (mode=github, repo matches, branch matches)
CANONICAL_REPO_URL = 'https://github.com/Project-X-Innovation/helix-cli.git'
CANONICAL_REPO = 'Project-X-Innovation/helix-cli'
CANONICAL_BRANCH = 'main'
version.ts reads package.json via relative path ../../package.json from dist/update/version.js — package.json IS included in tarball so this will work from npm install
package-lock.json lockfileVersion is 3 (npm v7+)
No .npmignore file — publish inclusion controlled entirely by files field
unknowns: Whether the npm scope @projectxinnovation exists on the npm registry and is controlled by the organization
Whether @projectxinnovation/helix-cli has ever been published to npm (version conflict risk)
The intended publish trigger: tag push, GitHub Release, manual dispatch, or other
Whether the npm org has Trusted Publishing already configured in the npm UI for this package/repo
Whether test files (*.test.js, *.test.d.ts) in dist/ should be excluded from the published tarball or if the current inclusion is acceptable
Whether the npm-based update path should use semver version comparison or a different versioning strategy
Whether the help text in src/index.ts line 52 ('Check for and apply updates from GitHub main') should be updated to reflect npm-based updates
